clusters.title: Sri Lanka ‘A’ bat themselves into strong position

versions.title: Sri Lanka ‘A’ bat themselves into strong position
versions.deck: Sri Lanka ‘A’ batted themselves into a strong position in the second unofficial 4-day test against New Zealand ‘A’ played at the Galle Cricket Stadium yesterday.

clusters.title: Battle heats up between Jaswar and Shirantha for NOCSL Presidency

versions.title: Battle heats up between Jaswar and Shirantha for NOCSL Presidency
versions.deck: The race for the National Olympic Committee of Sri Lanka (NOCSL) Presidency has intensified ahead of its crucial Annual General Meeting (AGM) scheduled for today, with three prominent administrators emerging as key contenders for the top post.

clusters.title: Petes run riot at Bamba

versions.title: Petes run riot at Bamba
versions.deck: St. Peter’s College produced a ruthless display to launch their Under-19 Schools Rugby League campaign with a thumping 67-0 victory over Mahanama College at Bambalapitiya yesterday evening.

clusters.title: Galle-Colombo clash will determine second finalist

versions.title: Galle-Colombo clash will determine second finalist
versions.deck: With Dambulla having already booked a place in the National Super League 4-day final, the battle for the second finalist spot will be between defending champions Galle and Colombo who meet at the Rangiri Dambulla Cricket Stadium today.

clusters.title: Sampath Bank tees off third consecutive year as RCGC Medal Tournament Title Sponsor

versions.title: Sampath Bank tees off third consecutive year as RCGC Medal Tournament Title Sponsor
versions.deck: Sampath Bank Private Banking has been named Title Sponsor of the latest edition of the Royal Colombo Golf Club Monthly Medal Golf Tournament, marking its third year of association with the event.

clusters.title: Wesley decimate PoW

versions.title: Wesley decimate PoW
versions.deck: Wesley College produced a dominant all-round performance to launch their 2026 rugby season under Kaizer Lye in emphatic fashion, thrashing newly promoted Prince of Wales College 69-12 in their Dialog Schools Under-19 Rugby League 2026 encounter at Havelock Park on Thursday.

clusters.title: Youthful SL lock horns with experienced NZ under 85kg

versions.title: Youthful SL lock horns with experienced NZ under 85kg
versions.deck: The first match between Sri Lanka XV and the visiting New Zealand Under-85kg side is set to take place at the Race Course ground tonight.

clusters.title: Asia-Pacific Ministers push food security, resilience amid global shocks at FAO meet

versions.title: Asia-Pacific Ministers push food security, resilience amid global shocks at FAO meet
versions.deck: Agriculture Ministers from across Asia and the Pacific have called for stronger regional cooperation to safeguard food security and build resilient agrifood systems, as geopolitical tensions and climate pressures intensify risks to global food supply chains.

clusters.title: SLASSCOM Women in Tech Conference 4.0 sets benchmark

versions.title: SLASSCOM Women in Tech Conference 4.0 sets benchmark
versions.deck: SLASSCOM Women in Tech Conference 4.0 reaffirmed its position as Sri Lanka’s premier platform for advancing women’s leadership in the digital economy.

clusters.title: World Intellectual Property Day commemorated

versions.title: World Intellectual Property Day commemorated
versions.deck: A special program organised to celebrate “World Intellectual Property Day,” which recognizes the value of human creativity, was held yesterday at the Duncan White Auditorium of the Youth Affairs and Sports Ministry.

clusters.title: CEB posts Rs. 39 b loss in 2025 despite tariff revisions

versions.title: CEB posts Rs. 39 b loss in 2025 despite tariff revisions
versions.deck: The Ceylon Electricity Board (CEB) remained under financial strain in 2025 despite cost-reflective tariff adjustments, the Central Bank said in its Annual Economic Review.

clusters.title: $ 6 b export gap and weak performance strains external stability: ADB

versions.title: $ 6 b export gap and weak performance strains external stability: ADB
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s export sector is operating well below potential, with an estimated $ 6 billion in unrealised export capacity, as weak trade performance continues to strain the country’s external position and weigh on currency stability.

clusters.title: Nestlé Lanka names Manav Sahni as new Chief, Bernie Stefan transitions to global role

versions.title: Nestlé Lanka names Manav Sahni as new Chief, Bernie Stefan transitions to global role
versions.deck: Nestlé Lanka Ltd. yesterday announced the appointment of Manav Sahni as Chairman and Managing Director, effective 1 May 2026, succeeding Bernie Stefan, who will transition to a new global role within the Nestlé Group.

clusters.title: CSE ends down 0.9% during week

versions.title: CSE ends down 0.9% during week
versions.deck: The Colombo stock market ended a cautious and volatile week down 0.9% or 205.7 points while the active S&P SL20 Index decreased by 59.3 points. During the week, the market witnessed a net foreign outflow of over Rs. 2.6 billion, taking the year-to-date net outflow to Rs. 24.9 billion

clusters.title: CPC posts Rs. 36.4 b profit in 2025

versions.title: CPC posts Rs. 36.4 b profit in 2025
versions.deck: The CPC reported a profit of Rs. 36.4 billion in 2025, marking its third consecutive year of positive performance, supported by the continuation of the cost-reflective pricing mechanism, the Central Bank said in its latest Annual Economic Review.

clusters.title: Daily FT-ICCSL webinar today: ‘Crisis or Opportunity – Sri Lanka’s Path During Middle East War’

versions.title: Daily FT-ICCSL webinar today: ‘Crisis or Opportunity – Sri Lanka’s Path During Middle East War’
versions.deck: The Daily FT – ACCA and The International Chamber of Commerce Sri Lanka, together with WIM, The Sri Lanka Institute of Directors, Employers Federation of Ceylon, Association of Accounting Technicians, MBA Alumni Association of the University of Colombo, BIMT Campus and the Pioneer Institute of Business and Technology will be conducting a webinar today titled “Crisis or Opportunity – Sri Lanka’s path During the Middle East War”.

clusters.title: Unpaid care work ‘social justice issue’ and growth constraint, says ILO

versions.title: Unpaid care work ‘social justice issue’ and growth constraint, says ILO
versions.deck: Unpaid care work remains one of the most overlooked yet critical barriers to gender equality and inclusive growth in Sri Lanka and across South Asia, a senior International Labour Organisation (ILO) official said, urging governments and partners to accelerate investment and policy reform in the care economy.

clusters.title: Demographic shift deepens as ageing, low fertility and inactivity reshape economy: Census 2024

versions.title: Demographic shift deepens as ageing, low fertility and inactivity reshape economy: Census 2024
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s latest Census of Population and Housing points to a structural shift in the country’s economic foundations, with ageing, low fertility, and weak labour force participation emerging as the most critical pressures on future growth and fiscal stability.

clusters.title: Trumpflation and recession

versions.title: Trumpflation and recession
versions.deck: Trumpflation has two components, inflation and stagflation, which ultimately lead to recession. It is a negative shock to the world economy.

clusters.title: Collapse of the world order and impotence of UN system

versions.title: Collapse of the world order and impotence of UN system
versions.deck: Failure of the UN system is a failure of member states to maintain and foster its objectives.

clusters.title: Emerging Asia could face increased food-cost pressures: Fitch

versions.title: Emerging Asia could face increased food-cost pressures: Fitch
versions.deck: Emerging markets in Asia would see rising cost pressure across agribusiness and food supply chains if a prolonged US-Iran war pushes fertiliser supply disruption deeper into the planting season, Fitch Ratings says.

clusters.title: From estates to equity: Reimagining Sri Lanka’s plantation sector

versions.title: From estates to equity: Reimagining Sri Lanka’s plantation sector
versions.deck: Picture a young woman on a Hatton estate, sitting in a digital hub, completing a software quality assurance task for a Chennai-based fintech firm.

clusters.title: 1Q exports edge up to $ 4.3 b, but March hit by Middle East tensions

versions.title: 1Q exports edge up to $ 4.3 b, but March hit by Middle East tensions
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s total export earnings registered a modest increase in the first quarter of 2026, reaching over $ 4.3 billion, up 1.6% year-on-year (YoY), according to the Sri Lanka Export Development Board (EDB).

clusters.title: Why should we protect Aswasuma and how to improve it ?

versions.title: Why should we protect Aswasuma and how to improve it ?
versions.deck: Conversations about Aswasuma have sparked again with much criticism in social media for the Government decision to increase the cash transfer allowance in April.

clusters.title: The good hypocrite!

versions.title: The good hypocrite!
versions.deck: This is about Political Hypocrisy. It is also about our ‘Leftist’ President who has made a turn to the ‘Right’. His ‘Opponents’ claim “they know better and could perform better’.

clusters.title: Why is it so difficult to invest in Sri Lanka?

versions.title: Why is it so difficult to invest in Sri Lanka?
versions.deck: Over the years, I have heard the same frustration from investors. They arrive with genuine interest, spend time exploring opportunities, hold meetings, visit sites, discuss structures, and then somewhere along the way their enthusiasm begins to drain away.

clusters.title: Safeguarding Jaffna College and Uduvil Girls’ College

versions.title: Safeguarding Jaffna College and Uduvil Girls’ College
versions.deck: A senior priest from the Jaffna Diocese of the Church of South India (JDCSI) has been arrested by police along with his two associates for allegedly sexually assaulting two young women.

clusters.title: Richmond at 150: Rekindling a tradition of refinement

versions.title: Richmond at 150: Rekindling a tradition of refinement
versions.deck: As Richmond College, Galle, marks its 150th anniversary on 1 May 2026, it does more than celebrate a passage of time. It stands at a reflective crossroads—between a proud past that shaped generations of distinguished citizens, and a future that urgently calls for the revival of values that once defined not only an institution,

clusters.title: Banks, Basel, compliance and governance

versions.title: Banks, Basel, compliance and governance
versions.deck: This is a sequel to the article published in this column on 9 April 2026, titled Epstein, Bank of America, JPMorgan and Deutsche (https://www.ft.lk/columns/Epstein-Bank-of-America-JPMorgan-and-Deutsche/4-790649).

clusters.title: My priority is to take Sri Lanka to top of the tree in world cricket – Kirsten

versions.title: My priority is to take Sri Lanka to top of the tree in world cricket – Kirsten
versions.deck: After a considerable period, Sri Lanka cricket has secured a top-quality coach to handle the national team in the form of Gary Kirsten, whose proven record speaks for itself, having guided India to win the 2011 Cricket World Cup, beating Sri Lanka in the final, and having taken his native South Africa to the top of the ICC Test rankings.

clusters.title: Govt. says President has not made request to remove SLC Head

versions.title: Govt. says President has not made request to remove SLC Head
versions.deck: The Government and Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) have said that they are not aware that President Anura Kumara Dissanayake has asked SLC President Shammi Silva to step down to facilitate the appointment of an interim committee.

clusters.title: Yamal scores but injured as Barcelona beat Celta to restore La Liga lead

versions.title: Yamal scores but injured as Barcelona beat Celta to restore La Liga lead
versions.deck: Lamine Yamal was injured while converting a penalty in Barcelona’s Spanish league win against Celta Vigo.
